title: New Media and Communication (English) Program Learning Outcomes
content:
New Media and Communication (English) Program Learning Outcomes

(1)

Students will comprehend the concepts, processes, and theories of new communication technologies.

(2)

Students will have a grasp of the evolving structure of media in the digital age.

(3)

Students will gain an interdisciplinary understanding of the social sciences and humanities, as well as a solid theoretical background in the field of new media.

(4)

Students will specialize in the technical and operational aspects of new media.

(5)

Students will be able to analyze the digital world and understand the social problems related to digitalization.

(6)

Students will possess digital and critical media literacy skills.

(7)

Students will be able to participate in digital intercultural communication.

(8)

Students will be able to create diverse digital content for multimedia environments.

(9)

Students will understand the structure of the media and communications sector.

(10)

Students will be able to exhibit professional competence in the field of new communication technologies.

(11)

Students will comprehend the new research paradigms and analyze data related to new media.

(12)

Students will be able to design multimedia applications.

(13)

Students will understand the processes involved in new media journalism and produce content accordingly.

(14)

Students will be able to follow and critically assess the worldwide events.

(15)

Students will be able to adapt to the dynamic nature of the digital age.

(16)

Students will be able to create scholarly content.

(17)

Students will gain a more sensitive perspective on social issues such as environmental problems and sustainability.

(18)

Students will be able to develop an innovative vision to effectively address emerging challenges and problems in the new media landscape.
